privileged_info_summary: The task uses Gretl to apply a Hodrick-Prescott filter with lambda=1600 (the standard for quarterly data) to the usa.gdt dataset, which contains US macroeconomic time series from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis. The data is real, not synthetic, with quarterly frequency starting from 1984 Q1. The task decomposes US Real GDP into trend and cyclical components, then calculates output gap as (cycle/trend)*100. The mathematical relationships are: GDP ≈ Trend + Cycle and output_gap = (cycle/trend)*100, verified through statsmodels implementation. Required outputs are a Gretl script (hp_analysis.inp), augmented dataset (usa_hp_decomposed.gdt), and text report (hp_report.txt) containing the smoothing parameter, output gap statistics (mean, std dev, negative count, and date of minimum).
